extracted_information: Stripe manages prorations for modified subscriptions to accurately reflect partial usage. Prorations are triggered by changes in price, billing period, quantity, or adding a trial period. They are applied to charges occurring ahead of the billing period, not usage-based billing. Stripe calculates prorations using the subscription's status at the time of update, based on the current billing period's start and end times. Users can preview prorations, manually create them, and control their behavior (default, immediate invoicing, or disabled) through API parameters. Credit prorations for downgrades/cancellations vary based on `billing_mode` (classic vs. flexible).

add_invoice_items.description: Used for manually creating prorations, pass with a negative `unit_amount`.

options.create_prorations: Default behavior; creates proration invoice items, invoiced later under certain conditions.
options.always_invoice: Calculates proration and immediately generates an invoice.
options.none: Disables prorations for the request; customer billed full amount at new price on next invoice.

billing_cycle_anchor.description: Resets the billing period to the current time, useful when disabling prorations for unpaid invoices.

options.classic: One approach for calculating credit prorations.
options.flexible: Another approach for calculating credit prorations, based on the last price billed for the subscription item.

proration_date.description: Sets a specific date for proration calculation to ensure consistency between preview and update.

examples.scenario: Upgrade from 10 USD/month to 20 USD/month halfway through billing period
examples.calculation: -5 USD (unused time on initial price) + 10 USD (remaining time on new price) = +5 USD additional charge.

examples.scenario: Downgrade 20 USD/month to 10 USD/month (after 10 days on 10 USD, then 10 days on 20 USD, then downgrade)

classic.description: Calculates credit based on current (20 USD) price, even if not fully paid.
classic.result: Invoice total: -3.34 USD (credits 1/3 of 20 USD: -6.67 USD; debits 1/3 of 10 USD: 3.33 USD).

flexible.description: Calculates credit based on the last price billed (10 USD).
flexible.result: Invoice total: 0 USD (credits 1/3 of 10 USD: 3.33 USD; debits 1/3 of 10 USD: 3.33 USD; credit and debit cancel out).

examples.scenario: Coupon applied to multiple subscription items (5 USD coupon on 25 USD total: 10 USD item + 20 USD item), then cancel 10 USD item.

classic.description: Distributes coupon evenly, then calculates refund.
classic.result: Invoice total: -2.50 USD (-0.5 * (10 USD price - 5 USD coupon)).

flexible.description: Reflects proportional discount applied to the canceled item.
flexible.result: Invoice total: -4.17 USD (-0.5 * (10 USD price - 1.66 USD discount amount)).

features.name: Automatic Proration Calculation
features.description: Stripe automatically calculates prorated charges when existing subscriptions are modified.

features.name: Proration Preview
features.description: Ability to preview an upcoming invoice with proration changes before applying them, using `create_preview` API.
features.api_usage: Stripe::Invoice.create_preview({ customer: 'cus_ID', subscription: 'sub_ID', subscription_details: { items: [...], proration_date: Time.now.to_i } })

features.name: Manual Proration Creation
features.description: Option to calculate prorations outside Stripe and add them to the subscription using `add_invoice_items` with a negative `unit_amount`.

features.name: Proration Behavior Control
features.description: Control how prorations are applied using the `proration_behavior` parameter (`create_prorations`, `always_invoice`, `none`).

features.name: Handling Unpaid Invoices
features.description: Options to avoid crediting for unpaid time, such as disabling prorations (`proration_behavior=none`) or resetting the billing period (`billing_cycle_anchor=now`).
features.considerations: Manual one-off invoice creation or voiding old invoices might be necessary.

features.name: Credit Prorations
features.description: Issued when customers downgrade or cancel subscription items before the end of their billing period, with calculations varying by `billing_mode`.

features.name: Discount Handling
features.description: Discounts are not applied to invoice items flagged as prorations (`prorations=true` are `discountable=false`). Previously applied subscription discounts affecting proration amounts are reflected in the proration invoice item's amount.

temporal_info.calculation_timing: Prorated amount is calculated as soon as the API updates the subscription, using the current billing period's start and end times.
temporal_info.proration_application: Prorations only apply to charges that occur ahead of the billing period.

references.description: Add invoice items to subscription schedules
Reset billing period to current time
references.context: Reset the billing period to the current time

references.description: Conditions for immediate payment on subscription changes
references.context: Immediate payment conditions for proration items
